      exact (proj (𝕜 := ℝ) (0 : Fin 1)).dsmooth, by simp⟩ (.single 0 1)) ∈
    Submodule.span ℝ {v : PreInternalTangentSpace x | ∃ p q : pointedPlots x,
      ∃ f : DSmoothMap (Eucl p.1.1) (Eucl q.1.1), ∃ w : Eucl p.1.1,
        v = PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p w - PreInternalTangentSpace.lof q (fderiv ℝ f 0 w)} := h''
  -- In the fine diffeology, the only relations come from smooth maps between Euclidean spaces.
  -- The difference of the two plots corresponds to the vector v - w.
  -- If this difference is in the span of the relations, then v - w must be zero.
  -- To see this, consider the evaluation map on the pre-tangent space that picks out the coefficient
  -- of the standard plot t ↦ x + t • u for any u.
  -- Since the diffeology is fine, every linear map is smooth, so we can use dual vectors to detect equality.
  have key : ∀ u : X, PreInternalTangentSpace.lof ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • u⟩, by
    refine ((dsmooth_add_left x).comp ?_).isPlot
    refine dsmooth_smul.comp (DSmooth.prod_mk ?_ dsmooth_const)
    exact (proj (𝕜 := ℝ) (0 : Fin 1)).dsmooth, by simp⟩ (.single 0 1) =
    PreInternalTangentSpace.lof ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • 0⟩, by
      refine ((dsmooth_add_left x).comp ?_).isPlot
      refine dsmooth_smul.comp (DSmooth.prod_mk ?_ dsmooth_const)
      exact (proj (𝕜 := ℝ) (0 : Fin 1)).dsmooth, by simp⟩ (.single 0 1) → u = 0 := by
    intro u hu
    -- Use the fact that in the fine diffeology, the map φ_u : X → ℝ defined by φ_u(y) = λ where y - x = λ • u (if u ≠ 0) is smooth,
    -- but more directly, we can use the universal property of the fine diffeology: any linear map is smooth.
    -- Consider the linear functional l : X → ℝ such that l(u) = 1 and l(v) = 0 for v in a complement (using Hahn-Banach if necessary).
    -- However, since we are over ℝ and in finite dimensions locally, we can avoid heavy machinery.
    -- Instead, note that if u ≠ 0, then the plot t ↦ x + t • u is not related to the constant plot unless u = 0.
    -- The relation submodule only identifies vectors that are linked by derivatives of smooth maps between Euclidean spaces.
    -- But the only way the two plots t ↦ x + t•v and t ↦ x + t•w can be related is if v = w, because the derivative of any smooth map
    -- f : ℝ → ℝ with f(0)=0 is just multiplication by f'(0), and reparametrizations cannot change the direction unless scaled.
    -- However, in the fine diffeology, all linear maps are smooth, so we can test against linear functionals.
    -- Specifically, for any linear functional l : X → ℝ, the composition l ∘ (t ↦ x + t•u) = l(x) + t•l(u) is a plot in ℝ.
    -- The tangent vector represented by this plot in ℝ is l(u) (in the standard tangent space of ℝ, which is ℝ itself).

    -- internalTangentMap l x (lof(plot_u)(e1)) = lof(plot_{l∘plot_u})(e1) = l(u) in T_{l(x)}ℝ ≅ ℝ.

    have h_eval : ∀ (l : X →ₗ[ℝ] ℝ), 
      internalTangentMap (l : X → ℝ) x (InternalTangentSpace.lof ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • u⟩, by
        refine ((dsmooth_add_left x).comp ?_).isPlot
        refine dsmooth_smul.comp (DSmooth.prod_mk ?_ dsmooth_const)
        exact (proj (𝕜 := ℝ) (0 : Fin 1)).dsmooth, by simp⟩ (.single 0 1)) =

        refine ((dsmooth_add_left (l x)).comp ?_).isPlot
        refine dsmooth_smul.comp (DSmooth.prod_mk ?_ dsmooth_const)
        exact (proj (𝕜 := ℝ) (0 : Fin 1)).dsmooth, by simp⟩ (.single 0 1) := by

    have h_zero : ∀ (l : X →ₗ[ℝ] ℝ), 
      internalTangentMap (l : X → ℝ) x (InternalTangentSpace.lof ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • u⟩, by
        refine ((dsmooth_add_left x).comp ?_).isPlot
        refine dsmooth_smul.comp (DSmooth.prod_mk ?_ dsmooth_const)
        exact (proj (𝕜 := ℝ) (0 : Fin 1)).dsmooth, by simp⟩ (.single 0 1)) =
      internalTangentMap (l : X → ℝ) x (InternalTangentSpace.lof ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • 0⟩, by
        refine ((dsmooth_add_left x).comp ?_).isPlot
        refine dsmooth_smul.comp (DSmooth.prod_mk ?_ dsmooth_const)
        exact (proj (𝕜 := ℝ) (0 : Fin 1)).dsmooth, by simp⟩ (.single 0 1)) := by
      rw [hu]

    -- So for every linear l, l(u) = 0.
    -- By the Hahn-Banach theorem (or simply because in a vector space, if all linear functionals vanish on u, then u=0),
    -- we conclude u = 0.
    -- However, we are in a general real vector space, not necessarily finite-dimensional, but the statement holds.
    -- In Lean, we can use the fact that the double dual separates points.
    have h_all_zero : ∀ (l : X →ₗ[ℝ] ℝ), l u = 0 := by
      intro l
      -- Compute both sides in the tangent space of ℝ
      -- For the plot t ↦ l x + t * l u, the tangent vector is l u (as an element of ℝ, via the canonical map)
      -- For the plot t ↦ l x + t * 0, the tangent vector is 0.
      -- And since the canonical map for ℝ is injective (we can prove it easily for ℝ), we get l u = 0.
      -- Let's prove injectivity for ℝ quickly:
      have inj_ℝ : Function.Injective (vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace (l x : ℝ)) := by
        -- For ℝ, the internal tangent space at any point is isomorphic to ℝ.

        -- This follows because the only relations in the pre-tangent space for ℝ are those coming from smooth maps f:ℝ→ℝ,

        -- a plot γ to γ'(0). This map is inverse to the canonical map.
        apply vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace_injective
        infer_instance
      -- Now, the tangent vector for the plot t ↦ l x + t * l u is vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace (l x) (l u)
      -- and for 0 it is vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace (l x) 0.
      -- By h_zero and h_eval, these are equal, so by inj_ℝ, l u = 0.
      have h1 : vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace (l x) (l u) = 
        internalTangentMap (l : X → ℝ) x (InternalTangentSpace.lof ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • u⟩, by
          refine ((dsmooth_add_left x).comp ?_).isPlot
          refine dsmooth_smul.comp (DSmooth.prod_mk ?_ dsmooth_const)
          exact (proj (𝕜 := ℝ) (0 : Fin 1)).dsmooth, by simp⟩ (.single 0 1)) := by
        -- This is by definition of vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace for ℝ and the action of internalTangentMap
        -- But actually, by the definition of vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace, for ℝ it is:
        -- vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace a r = InternalTangentSpace.lof ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ a + t 0 * r⟩, ...⟩ (.single 0 1)
        -- And internalTangentMap l x applied to the plot for u gives exactly the plot for l u, so they are equal by definition.

      have h2 : vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace (l x) 0 = 
        internalTangentMap (l : X → ℝ) x (InternalTangentSpace.lof ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • 0⟩, by
          refine ((dsmooth_add_left x).comp ?_).isPlot
          refine dsmooth_smul.comp (DSmooth.prod_mk ?_ dsmooth_const)
          exact (proj (𝕜 := ℝ) (0 : Fin 1)).dsmooth, by simp⟩ (.single 0 1)) := by

    -- In Lean, we can use the following lemma: if for all l : X →ₗ[ℝ] ℝ, l u = 0, then u = 0.
    -- This is a standard result in linear algebra.

  have h_diff : InternalTangentSpace.lof ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • (v - w)⟩, by
    refine ((dsmooth_add_left x).comp ?_).isPlot
    refine dsmooth_smul.comp (DSmooth.prod_mk ?_ dsmooth_const)
    exact (proj (𝕜 := ℝ) (0 : Fin 1)).dsmooth, by simp⟩ (.single 0 1) =
    InternalTangentSpace.lof ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • 0⟩, by
      refine ((dsmooth_add_left x).comp ?_).isPlot
      refine dsmooth_smul.comp (DSmooth.prod_mk ?_ dsmooth_const)
      exact (proj (𝕜 := ℝ) (0 : Fin 1)).dsmooth, by simp⟩ (.single 0 1) := by

    -- But from h''', we know that the difference of the two original plots is in the relation submodule.
    -- However, note that the plot for v - w is not directly the difference, but we can relate them.
    -- Actually, we have:
    -- lof(plot_v) - lof(plot_w) = lof(plot_{v-w}) - lof(plot_0) ??? Not exactly.
    -- Let's compute the difference in the pre-tangent space:

    -- Instead, we use the linearity of the canonical map for the vector space structure.
    -- But wait, we haven't proven that the canonical map is linear yet! Actually, we have: vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace is defined as a linear map.
    -- So by linearity, vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x (v - w) = vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x v - vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x w.
    -- And h says that vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x v = vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x w, so their difference is zero.

      rw [← sub_eq_zero]
      convert congr_arg (· - ·) h using 1
      -- But actually, since it's a linear map, we have:
      have := (vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x).map_sub v w
      rw [h, sub_self, zero_sub] at this
      exact this.symm
    exact h_vw
  -- Now apply key to u = v - w
  have h_vw_zero : v - w = 0 := key (v - w) h_diff
  exact sub_eq_zero.1 h_vw_zero

obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := h
  have : ∀ p ∈ s, ∃ (p₁ p₂ : pointedPlots x) (f : DSmoothMap _ _) (u), p = PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p₁ u - PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p₂ (fderiv ℝ f 0 u) := by
    intro p hp
    exact Submodule.span_induction hp (by tauto) (by tauto) (by tauto) (by tauto)
  -- In the fine diffeology, plots are smooth maps from Euclidean spaces, so we can use standard calculus.
  -- The key idea: if two vectors give the same tangent vector, then their difference is zero.
  let φ_v : Eucl 1 → X := fun t ↦ x + t 0 • v
  let φ_w : Eucl 1 → X := fun t ↦ x + t 0 • w
  have hv : IsPlot φ_v := by
    apply isPlot_of_fine
    continuity
    differentiable
  have hw : IsPlot φ_w := by
    apply isPlot_of_fine
    continuity
    differentiable
  let p_v : pointedPlots x := ⟨⟨1, φ_v⟩, hv, by simp⟩
  let p_w : pointedPlots x := ⟨⟨1, φ_w⟩, hw, by simp⟩
  -- The difference of the two generators lies in the span, so it must be expressible via reparametrizations.
  -- But in a fine diffeological vector space, the only way this can happen is if v = w.
  have key : PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p_v (.single 0 1) - PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p_w (.single 0 1) ∈ Submodule.span ℝ
    {v : PreInternalTangentSpace x | ∃ p q : pointedPlots x,
      ∃ f : DSmoothMap (Eucl p.1.1) (Eucl q.1.1), ∃ w : Eucl p.1.1,
        v = PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p w - PreInternalTangentSpace.lof q (fderiv ℝ f 0 w)} := hs
  -- Now use the fact that in the fine diffeology, the tangent space is canonically isomorphic to X.
  -- Specifically, the map v ↦ [t ↦ x + t•v] is injective.
  -- Suppose v ≠ w; then the paths φ_v and φ_w have different derivatives at 0, so they cannot be equivalent.
  -- To make this rigorous, consider the linear functional on X given by any continuous linear map.
  -- Since X is fine, all linear maps are smooth, so we can test against them.
  suffices : v - w = 0 by simpa
  apply eq_of_forall_eq_zero
  intro f hf
  -- f : X →L[ℝ] ℝ is a continuous linear functional; since X is fine, it's smooth.
  have hf' : DSmooth f := by
    apply dsmooth_of_fine
    exact hf.continuous
  -- Apply the tangent map of f to both sides; it should preserve the equality.
  have h_tangent : internalTangentMap f x (vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x v) =
    internalTangentMap f x (vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x w) := by
    rw [h]
  -- Compute both sides explicitly.
  have comp_v : f ∘ φ_v = fun t ↦ f x + t 0 * f v := by
    ext t; simp [φ_v, map_add, map_smul]
  have comp_w : f ∘ φ_w = fun t ↦ f x + t 0 * f w := by
    ext t; simp [φ_w, map_add, map_smul]

  have tv_v : internalTangentMap f x (vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x v) = f v • (InternalTangentSpace.lof
    ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ f x + t 0 * f v⟩, (hf'.comp hv.dsmooth).isPlot, by simp⟩ (.single 0 1)) := by
    sorry
  -- Instead, use a more direct approach: in ℝ, the internal tangent space at any point is canonically ℝ.
  -- And the map vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace for ℝ is an isomorphism.
  have iso_ℝ : InternalTangentSpace (f x : ℝ) ≃ₗ[ℝ] ℝ := by
    -- This is a known result for ℝ, but we don't have it yet. However, we can construct it.
    -- For ℝ with fine diffeology, the internal tangent space at any point is ℝ.
    -- The map sending r to the class of t ↦ (f x) + t*r is an isomorphism.
    apply LinearEquiv.ofBijective
    · intro r
      exact InternalTangentSpace.lof ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ f x + t 0 * r⟩, by
        apply isPlot_of_fine
        continuity
        differentiable, by simp⟩ (.single 0 1)
    · constructor
      · intro r s hrs
        -- Injectivity for ℝ: if two paths t ↦ a + t*r and t ↦ a + t*s give the same tangent vector, then r = s.
        -- This follows because the only reparametrizations that preserve the basepoint are linear near 0.
        sorry
      · intro z
        -- Surjectivity: every tangent vector comes from some path, and in ℝ all plots are smooth functions,
        -- so the derivative at 0 gives the scalar.
        sorry
  -- Given the complexity, and since we're in a fine diffeological vector space,
  -- we can use the fact that the equivalence relation identifies two vectors iff they are equal.
  -- Specifically, the relation generated by reparametrizations cannot identify v and w unless v = w,
  -- because any reparametrization f with f(0)=0 has fderiv ℝ f 0 = λ t, c*t for some c,
  -- and then the path t ↦ x + (c*t)*v would be equivalent to t ↦ x + t*(c*v).
  -- But our paths are t ↦ x + t*v and t ↦ x + t*w, so for them to be equivalent, we need v = w.
  -- More formally, suppose there is a chain of relations connecting them.
  -- The simplest case is direct: there exists a plot p, a reparametrization f, such that
  --   lof p_v (.single 0 1) - lof p_w (.single 0 1) = lof p u - lof (reparam p f) (fderiv f 0 u)
  -- But in our case, both p_v and p_w are plots from Eucl 1.
  -- The only way this can happen is if v = w.
  -- To avoid the complexity of chains, note that in the fine diffeology, the internal tangent space
  -- is isomorphic to the usual tangent space, which for a vector space is the space itself.
  -- Since we don't have that theorem yet, we prove injectivity directly.
  -- Consider the difference v - w. If it's nonzero, then there exists a continuous linear functional f
  -- such that f(v - w) ≠ 0 (by Hahn-Banach, but we don't have that in Lean yet).
  -- However, in finite dimensions this is true, but X might be infinite-dimensional.
  -- But in the fine diffeology, even for infinite-dimensional spaces, the tangent space is the space itself.
  -- Alternative approach: use the definition of the equivalence relation.
  -- The element lof p_v (.single 0 1) - lof p_w (.single 0 1) is in the span of differences of the form
  --   lof p u - lof q (fderiv f 0 u)
  -- where q = reparam p f.
  -- In particular, for the specific plots p_v and p_w, if they were related by a reparametrization,
  -- then there would be a smooth f : Eucl 1 → Eucl 1 with f(0)=0 such that
  --   φ_w = φ_v ∘ f
  -- i.e., x + t•w = x + f(t)•v for all t, so t•w = f(t)•v.
  -- Taking derivative at 0: w = f'(0)•v.
  -- But also, for the tangent vectors to be equal, we need the generator for v to equal that for w,
  -- which would require that the class of (.single 0 1) in p_v equals that in p_w.
  -- In the quotient, this means that (.single 0 1) for p_v minus (.single 0 1) for p_w is in the span.
  -- The only relations are from reparametrizations, so there must be a chain.
  -- However, in the fine diffeology, the only plots that matter are the linear ones for the purpose of tangent vectors.
  -- Given the time, and since this is a standard result, we use the following trick:
  -- Define a linear map from PreInternalTangentSpace x to X that sends lof p u to (fderiv ℝ p.2 0) u.
  -- This map vanishes on the relations, so it descends to the quotient.
  -- Then, applying this map to both sides gives v = w.
  let eval : PreInternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] X :=
    toModule ℝ (pointedPlots x) X fun p ↦ fderiv ℝ p.2 0

    intro p q f u
    simp only [toModule_lof, sub_eq_add_neg, map_add, map_neg, neg_sub]
    rw [← fderiv.comp, ← fderiv_const]
    congr
    ext t
    simp [Function.comp]
  have eval_descends : Submodule.span ℝ
    {v : PreInternalTangentSpace x | ∃ p q : pointedPlots x,
      ∃ f : DSmoothMap (Eucl p.1.1) (Eucl q.1.1), ∃ w : Eucl p.1.1,
        v = PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p w - PreInternalTangentSpace.lof q (fderiv ℝ f 0 w)} ≤
    LinearMap.ker eval := by
    apply Submodule.span_le
    rintro _ ⟨p, q, f, w, rfl⟩
    exact eval_rel p q f w
  let eval_tangent : InternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] X :=
    Submodule.mkQ_ker_lift (Submodule.span ℝ _) eval eval_descends
  have eval_tangent_lof : ∀ (p : pointedPlots x) (u : Eucl p.1.1),
      eval_tangent (InternalTangentSpace.lof p u) = fderiv ℝ p.2 0 u := by
    intro p u
    dsimp [eval_tangent, InternalTangentSpace.lof]
    simp only [Submodule.mkQ_ker_lift_mkQ, toModule_lof]
  have ev_v : eval_tangent (vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x v) = v := by
    dsimp [vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace]
    rw [eval_tangent_lof]
    -- Compute fderiv of t ↦ x + t 0 • v at 0.
    have : fderiv ℝ (fun t : Eucl 1 ↦ x + t 0 • v) 0 = (fun t ↦ t 0 • v) := by
      apply HasFDerivAt.fderiv
      apply HasFDerivAt.add_const
      apply HasFDerivAt.smul
      · apply HasFDerivAt.proj
      · apply hasFDerivAt_const
    rw [this]
    simp
  have ev_w : eval_tangent (vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x w) = w := by
    dsimp [vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace]
    rw [eval_tangent_lof]
    have : fderiv ℝ (fun t : Eucl 1 ↦ x + t 0 • w) 0 = (fun t ↦ t 0 • w) := by
      apply HasFDerivAt.fderiv
      apply HasFDerivAt.add_const
      apply HasFDerivAt.smul
      · apply HasFDerivAt.proj
      · apply hasFDerivAt_const
    rw [this]
    simp
  rw [h] at ev_v
  rw [ev_v, ev_w]

have h' : InternalTangentSpace.lof ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • v⟩, (by
      refine ((dsmooth_add_left x).comp ?_).isPlot
      refine dsmooth_smul.comp (DSmooth.prod_mk ?_ dsmooth_const)
      exact (proj (𝕜 := ℝ) (0 : Fin 1)).dsmooth), by simp⟩ (.single 0 1) =
    InternalTangentSpace.lof ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • w⟩, (by
      refine ((dsmooth_add_left x).comp ?_).isPlot
      refine dsmooth_smul.comp (DSmooth.prod_mk ?_ dsmooth_const)
      exact (proj (𝕜 := ℝ) (0 : Fin 1)).dsmooth), by simp⟩ (.single 0 1) := by
    exact h
  rw [InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp_apply, InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp_apply] at h'
  · simp at h'
    have : v = w := by
      apply_fun (fun z ↦ z 0) at h'
      simpa using h'
    exact this

    apply IsFineDiffeology.eq_zero_of_tangent_zero
    refine Submodule.Quotient.eq_zero_iff.1 ?_
    convert h using 1
    · ext
      simp [Subtype.val_eq_coe]
    · ext
      simp [Subtype.val_eq_coe]

have h' : InternalTangentSpace.lof ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • v⟩, (by
      refine ((dsmooth_add_left x).comp ?_).isPlot
      refine dsmooth_smul.comp (DSmooth.prod_mk ?_ dsmooth_const)
      exact (proj (𝕜 := ℝ) (0 : Fin 1)).dsmooth), by simp⟩ (.single 0 1) =

    have eval_w : eval (PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p_w (.single 0 1)) = w := by
      simp [eval, p_w, fderiv_add_const, fderiv_smul_const, fderiv_id']
    have eval_rel : ∀ (p q : pointedPlots x) (f : DSmoothMap (Eucl p.1.1) (Eucl q.1.1)) (w : Eucl p.1.1),
        eval (PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p w - PreInternalTangentSpace.lof q (fderiv ℝ f 0 w)) = 0 := by
      intro p q f w
      simp [eval, fderiv_comp]
      <;> ring
    have eval_zero : eval (lof p_v (.single 0 1) - lof p_w (.single 0 1)) = 0 := by
      apply Submodule.span_induction h_in_span
      · intro z hz
        obtain ⟨p, q, f, w, rfl⟩ := hz
        exact eval_rel p q f w
      · simp
      · intro a b _ _; simp
    simp [eval_v, eval_w, sub_eq_zero] at eval_zero
    exact eval_zero
  exact key
